segments.text: After you watch this video, I want you
to mark this moment as the first day of
your new Instagram account. Maybe you've
tried posting before, but you always
fizzle out. Maybe you haven't posted,
but you've been thinking about it for
too long now. Or maybe you're currently
living your dream life and posting
content, but now you're in dire need of
a rebrand. I've personally been living,
to be honest, my dream life for the past
year. I really can't complain, but I'm
now currently doing my third or fourth
rebrand revamp on this Instagram
account. So, let me share exactly how
I'm doing it and then hopefully it
inspires you to take the same steps
because it's not all that hard. Okay.
Number one, when you start posting, do
not listen to all the cookie cutter
content guru advice. Do not go and batch
10 videos, 30 videos, and then just
think you're going to get it right on
the very first shot and then that's it.
When you're starting out, you're going
to have no idea what you're going to do.
And the only way to actually enjoy the
content you're making is by taking it
day by day. So only make one or two
videos per day, edit it yourself or give
it to an editor and just have that
constant feedback until you make
something that you actually like. And
then you can start batching more and
more content pieces like 10 or 30 in a
day if you wanted to. But first, you got
to figure out what sticks and what you
like and what your audience likes.
Should have been step one. You're going
to want to actually figure out why you
want to be building your personal brand
in the first place. Is this purely a
hobby or is this more of a business or
side hustle for you? you're going to
land somewhere on this spectrum. Um,
everyone does. So, make sure you're
ultra intentional about where you want
to be. If you want to treat it like a
business and one day you want to be
earning some extra money, a side hustle,
or make it your full-time thing, then
start with that in mind. And you want to
make sure you start with a game plan
because you can make, you know, a couple
thousand dollars in the first couple
months of you posting content if you
want to. If it's just a hobby and a
creative outlet and you want to just
post some fun videos, then do that. And
then you don't really need too much of a
game plan and you can have fun with it.
Either way, you just want to be ultra
clear of what you want and then have a
plan and stick to the plan and you can
just get after it and start making
content and not overthink it. Okay, good
luck.
